dimensions.understanding: Restates the problem, constraints, and success condition accurately.
dimensions.examples: Builds clarifying examples and edge cases before designing the algorithm.
dimensions.brute_force: Finds a correct baseline before optimizing.
dimensions.pattern_detection: Identifies the governing invariant or reusable pattern.
dimensions.algorithm_design: Produces a correct optimized approach with the right data flow.
dimensions.complexity_analysis: Explains time and space tradeoffs precisely.
dimensions.implementation: Translates the plan into code without breaking invariants.
dimensions.communication: Communicates decisions, alternatives, and failure modes clearly.

labels.5: Adequate but inconsistent under interview pressure.

interview_dimensions.understanding: Problem reading, scope control, and clarification quality.
interview_dimensions.communication: Structure, precision, and interviewer alignment while speaking.
interview_dimensions.algorithm: Algorithm quality, invariant selection, and decision-making.

hint_levels.0: Solved without hints. Silence — allow thinking.
hint_levels.2: Needed one directional hint (e.g. ask for another example or edge case).
hint_levels.3: Needed multiple directional hints (e.g. "what information is being reused?").
hint_levels.4: Needed pattern-level guidance (e.g. ask for the invariant, without naming the pattern).
hint_levels.5: Needed step-level algorithm guidance (e.g. ask for a proof, or reveal one logical step — never the full algorithm).
hint_levels.7: Solution was effectively revealed. Allowed only if the student explicitly requests it, or remains stuck after multiple genuine proof attempts.

hint_mastery_discount.description: Skill-mastery evidence weight for a primary-validation solve, by hint_level_used. Hint 0-2 counts full; 3-4 counts at half weight; 5-7 counts as an attempt only (never enough to master).

revision_policy.description: Revision progression is state-based: a problem is mastered only after four successful active-recall revisions. Time passing only makes a revision due; it never advances mastery.
revision_policy.failure_effect: A failed revision does not advance stage. It is retried the next day at the same stage.
revision_policy.maintenance_failure_effect: A failed quarterly maintenance check restores the problem to ACTIVE at stage 3.
revision_policy.revision_backlog_note: New problem solving proceeds while the number of due revisions is at or below revision_backlog_threshold; past it, recall takes priority until the backlog drains. Reactivations and quarterly maintenance are never deferred. 0 restores strict revision-first order.

labels.7: Passable active recall with minimal prompting.

dimensions.concept_recall: Explains the intuition without being led to the pattern.
dimensions.invariant_recall: States the invariant or correctness lever precisely.
dimensions.algorithm_reconstruction: Reconstructs the algorithm from the invariant.
dimensions.implementation: Writes or describes an implementation that preserves the invariant.
dimensions.hint_dependency: Requires minimal or no hints during recall.
dimensions.implementation_blueprint: Explains state, initialization, loop bounds, previous-state needs, answer maintenance, and return value before coding.
dimensions.code_from_memory: Writes correct implementation from memory before the revision can pass.

revision_evaluation.pass_rule: A revision counts only when pattern, state, transition, complexity, implementation blueprint, code from memory, dry run, edge cases, and interview discussion are all satisfactory with minimal or no hints.

revision_triggers: Any failed active recall keeps the same revision stage and schedules the problem for tomorrow.
Failure on a later problem caused by an earlier concept should restore that prerequisite problem to ACTIVE revision.
Quarterly maintenance struggles should restore a mastered problem to ACTIVE at stage 3.
Repeated misses on the same pattern should update thinking_profile.gaps.

skill_mastery.description: A skill is mastered when its primary validation problem is solved at or above minimum_primary_weighted_score AND at least one reinforcement problem for that skill is solved (any passing score). This mirrors improvement #11: the mentor unlocks new material on skill mastery, not merely on problem completion.

stage_mastery.description: A stage is mastered once every skill in that stage (see curriculum/stages.json `skills`) is mastered.

algorithm_thinking.description: Independent session score for algorithm discovery and reasoning. Never mix with implementation engineering mistakes.
algorithm_thinking.weights_note: Used by migrate_progress_payload to backfill algorithm_thinking_score from 6 algorithm-side thinking dimensions (x2.5 to the 0-10 scale).

dimensions.pattern_recognition: Identifies the relevant invariant or reasoning family.
dimensions.state_design: Defines the right state, invariant, or data representation.
dimensions.transition: Derives the recurrence, greedy decision, or update rule correctly.
dimensions.complexity: Explains time and space from the algorithm structure.

implementation_engineering.description: Independent session score for translating a known algorithm into correct code.

dimensions.initialization: Initial values satisfy the state definition, including index 0.
dimensions.loop_bounds: Loop start, end, direction, and inclusivity are correct.
dimensions.previous_state: Previous values are preserved when an update depends on old state.
dimensions.update_ordering: State variables update in an order that preserves the invariant.
dimensions.answer_maintenance: Global/local answer ownership and update point are correct.
dimensions.return_value: The returned variable exactly matches the problem requirement.
dimensions.edge_cases: Code is dry-run against boundary and adversarial cases.

readiness.description: F23: system-derived interview-readiness estimator. Thresholds are informational only - readiness gates nothing.
